prose:
Method–provenance summary (reproducible):
Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f+ ↔ electrophilic attack; f– ↔ nucleophilic attack) for pyridine, with highlight on the most electrophilic site.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted (PySCF backend).
Geometry provenance: fixed ‑ used the user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz without additional optimisation (single-point population analyses on that geometry).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge / multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet); cation/anion calculations employed charge ±1, multiplicity 2 as required by the finite-difference Fukui protocol.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF v2 back-end; Mulliken charges).
Convergence & caveats: SCF for the N, N+1 and N–1 states all reported convergence; integrity.checks report status "ok" (trustworthy = true). 3 warnings were issued by the engine and are reproduced verbatim below.  
Live log: the run was launched with
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out …/live.json
and streamed to a live file fukui_20260703-204829.out in the caller’s working directory (path was displayed at launch and can be tailed for full SCF / population details).

Result
The largest electrophilic Fukui function value (f+) was found on atom 1 (N) of pyridine:
  f+(N1) = 0.48422572 (dimensionless)

Warnings (verbatim from tool):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

No additional warnings were reported; integrity.trustworthy = true.

All files produced (live.json, *.out log, and the plotted Fukui surface PNG) are available under /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/ and the timestamped subdirectory 20260703-204829_pyridine_fukui_reactivity.  The run can be fully reproduced by rerunning the CLI command above on the same .xyz geometry.
